title: Promotion methods
summary: 9609 AS — advertising, sales promotion, PR, direct and digital promotion with budget and audience fit.
learningObjectives: Compare advertising, sales promotion, PR, and direct methods.
Select promotion mix for target segments and PLC stage.
Evaluate cost-effectiveness and digital vs traditional.

simpleExplanation.title: Getting the message out
simpleExplanation.summary: Promotion communicates value and persuades purchase — through mass advertising, short-term incentives, trusted PR, or targeted digital campaigns.
steps: Advertising: paid mass media, brand building.
PR: unpaid favourable publicity and credibility.

flashcards.back: Mass media advertising (TV, radio, print) — brand awareness.
Targeted promotion: sales promotion, direct mail, sponsorship, digital ads.
Discounts, loyalty cards, free samples, competitions — short-term sales boost.
Third-party credibility; lower direct cost than advertising.
Email, SMS, personalised offers — measurable response rates.
Targeting, analytics, lower entry cost for SMEs, social sharing.
Heavy awareness advertising; inform early adopters.
Sales promotion and loyalty to defend share; brand refresh.

sections.content: **Promotion methods** (3.3.5) must match **target segment** (3.1.6), **product type**, and **PLC stage**. Case firms with **limited budget** should favour **digital/BTL** over national TV.

**Advertising** — builds brand over time; expensive; hard to measure precisely.

**Sales promotion** — immediate sales spike; may damage premium image if overused.

**Public relations** — press releases, events, CSR stories; builds trust.

**Direct/digital** — email, social ads, influencers; data-driven targeting.

sections.question: A local organic bakery targets health-conscious urban professionals. Budget is small. Recommend a promotion mix.
sections.solution:
**Social media (Instagram/TikTok):** Food photography, behind-the-scenes — **low cost**, visual product, reaches target demographic.

**Sampling at farmers' markets:** **Sales promotion** — trial reduces purchase risk.

**Local food blogger PR:** **Third-party endorsement** cheaper than print ads.

**Email loyalty programme:** **Direct marketing** — repeat purchases, measurable.

**Avoid:** National TV — wrong audience and budget. Mix is **BTL/digital-heavy** and coherent with niche positioning.

sections.content: Always state **objective** (awareness vs conversion) before choosing tools — examiners mark strategic fit, not tool lists.
